title: Fort Wayne-based The Ferguson Agency details leadership changes
description:
The agency announced Thursday that Principal John Ferguson has moved from CEO to chairman, while Kyle Martin is being promoted from president to CEO.

title: Public school group expects record number of school referendums
description:
The Indiana Coalition for Public Education anticipates as many as 100 public school districts could ask voters for new money through local property tax referendums this fall.

title: University of Saint Francis president to step down
description:
Lance Richey has led the Fort Wayne-based university since May of 2023, when he took over on an interim basis from former President Rev. Eric Zimmer. He was formally named president in 2024.

title: Bunge opens massive soy protein facility in Shelby County
description:
Bunge leaders touted the new facility, saying it is the largest soy protein concentrate plant in North America and the first one built in the United States in more than 40 years.

title: Visit Fort Wayne launches Allen County Sports Commission
description:
The commission, which will be comprised of business and community leaders, is designed to formalize and elevate Visit Fort Wayne's existing sports destination initiatives.

title: Purdue, Cook Medical sign research and collaboration agreement
description:
Purdue University and Bloomington-based Cook Medical are teaming up in an effort to advance innovative medical technologies and bolster the biomedical engineering workforce pipeline.

title: March Madness tournaments will expand to 76 teams each starting next season
description:
The new, 76-team brackets will jam eight extra games — for a total of 12 involving 24 teams — into the front half of the first week of the men's and the women's tournaments.

title: Boone County considering moratorium on data center development
description: The Boone County Area Plan Commission discussed the idea at Wednesday’s meeting. They unanimously voted to forward a favorable recommendation for the moratorium to the Boone County Commissioners, who have final approval.
